title: Renta Corporación Real Estate And 2 Other European Penny Stocks To Watch
content:
The European markets have experienced a positive shift, with the pan-European STOXX Europe 600 Index rising by 3.92% amid hopes for a shorter conflict in the Middle East. For those interested in exploring opportunities beyond well-known companies, penny stocks can offer intriguing prospects despite their somewhat outdated label. Typically representing smaller or newer firms, these stocks provide potential growth at lower price points, and when backed by strong financials, they can present less risk than traditionally perceived.

Top 10 Penny Stocks In Europe

Name Share Price Market Cap Financial Health Rating Ariston Holding (BIT:ARIS) €3.80 €1.31B ★★★★★★ Orthex Oyj (HLSE:ORTHEX) €4.75 €84.35M ★★★★★★ Angler Gaming (NGM:ANGL) SEK3.60 SEK269.95M ★★★★★★ Angler Gaming (DB:0QM) €0.31 €246.7M ★★★★★★ Verkkokauppa.com Oyj (HLSE:VERK) €3.27 €147.41M ★★★★★☆ Nurminen Logistics Oyj (HLSE:NLG1V) €0.83 €67.01M ★★★★★★ High (ENXTPA:HCO) €3.26 €63.18M ★★★★★★ Deceuninck (ENXTBR:DECB) €2.02 €279.26M ★★★★★★ Netgem (ENXTPA:ALNTG) €0.71 €23.78M ★★★★★★ Pharming Group (ENXTAM:PHARM) €1.4145 €992.53M ★★★★★★

Click here to see the full list of 283 stocks from our European Penny Stocks screener.

Let's take a closer look at a couple of our picks from the screened companies.

Renta Corporación Real Estate

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Renta Corporación Real Estate, S.A. is a real estate company focused on acquiring, refurbishing, and selling properties in Barcelona and Madrid, Spain, with a market cap of €300.01 million.

Operations: The company's revenue is derived from two main segments: Proprietary, generating €2.54 million, and Transactional Business, contributing €42.84 million.

Market Cap: €30M

Renta Corporación Real Estate, S.A. has shown financial resilience with its debt to equity ratio significantly reduced over five years and its debt well covered by operating cash flow. The company possesses more cash than total debt, ensuring short-term liabilities are comfortably managed. Despite a volatile share price and negative earnings growth last year, Renta's revenue increased to €45.38 million in 2025 from €25.72 million the previous year, though net income decreased to €2.51 million due to large one-off items impacting results. Its management team is experienced, but low return on equity and declining profit margins remain concerns for investors.

Story Continues

BME:REN Financial Position Analysis as at Apr 2026

S.C. Comcm

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: S.C. Comcm S.A. is a Romanian company involved in the manufacture and sale of concrete, with a market capitalization of RON67.77 million.

Operations: The company generates revenue of RON0.62 million from its operations in Romania.

Market Cap: RON67.77M

S.C. Comcm S.A., a Romanian concrete manufacturer, operates with a market capitalization of RON67.77 million and reported revenue of RON0.62 million, classifying it as pre-revenue. Despite being debt-free and having assets exceeding liabilities, the company remains unprofitable with a negative return on equity of -0.39%. Its share price has been highly volatile recently, although shareholder dilution has not been significant in the past year. Earnings results for 2025 showed reduced revenue compared to the previous year and a net loss of RON0.34 million following last year's net income, indicating financial challenges amidst stable weekly volatility relative to most Romanian stocks.

Click here to discover the nuances of S.C. Comcm with our detailed analytical financial health report. Explore historical data to track S.C. Comcm's performance over time in our past results report.BVB:CMCM Financial Position Analysis as at Apr 2026

MEMSCAP

Simply Wall St Financial Health Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: MEMSCAP, S.A. offers micro-electro-mechanical systems (MEMS) solutions for the aerospace and defense, optical communications, medical, and biomedical markets globally with a market cap of €32.55 million.

Operations: The company's revenue is primarily derived from its aerospace segment at €8.81 million, followed by the optical communications sector at €1.63 million, and the medical field contributing €1.47 million.

Market Cap: €32.55M

MEMSCAP, S.A. has a market cap of €32.55 million and primarily generates revenue from its aerospace segment (€8.81 million). Despite stable weekly volatility, the company faces challenges with negative earnings growth over the past year and declining profit margins (7.5% from 10.9%). However, MEMSCAP maintains a strong financial position with short-term assets (€12 million) exceeding liabilities and more cash than debt, which is well-covered by operating cash flow (1370.7%). The price-to-earnings ratio of 36.4x is below industry averages, suggesting potential value despite recent sales decline to €11.99 million for fiscal year 2025 from €12.66 million previously.
